title: sim: Print hierarchy for failed assertions
body:
Take e.g.

```
module top();
	a a_();
endmodule

module a();
	 b b_();
endmodule

module b();
	always @(*)
		assert(0);
endmodule
```

as input to sim (`read_verilog -sv sample.sv; hierarchy -top top; proc; sim -assert`).

You will now get

```
4. Executing SIM pass (simulate the circuit).
Simulating cycle 0.
Simulating cycle 1.
Failed assertion $assert$sample.sv:10$5 at
    sample.sv:10.13-11.12
  in instance b_ of module b at
    sample.sv:6.5-6.9
  in instance a_ of module a at
    sample.sv:2.4-2.8
ERROR: Assertion top.a_.b_.$assert$sample.sv:10$5 (sample.sv:10.13-11.12) failed.
```
code_blocks:
4. Executing SIM pass (simulate the circuit).
Simulating cycle 0.
Simulating cycle 1.
Failed assertion $assert$sample.sv:10$5 at
    sample.sv:10.13-11.12
  in instance b_ of module b at
    sample.sv:6.5-6.9
  in instance a_ of module a at
    sample.sv:2.4-2.8
ERROR: Assertion top.a_.b_.$assert$sample.sv:10$5 (sample.sv:10.13-11.12) failed.
